answer:
The correct answer is \boxed{I}.  
Statement (ii) is true because diagnostic testing in the Box-Jenkins approach checks residuals for autocorrelation, heteroscedasticity, and non-normality.  
Statements (iii) and (iv) are also true: appropriate models yield residuals with no additional structure in the ACF/PACF, and overfitting tests should show insignificant coefficients on extra variables.  
Statement (i) is false because diagnostic tests primarily detect if the model is too small (underfitted), not if it is too large.
